Source: Windsurf Documentation
URL: https://docs.windsurf.com
Fetched: 2026-03-22

# Windsurf Documentation

## Core Description
Windsurf is a next-generation AI IDE built to keep you in the flow. "Tomorrow's editor, today." Emphasizes AI-powered coding assistance while maintaining user productivity and workflow continuity.

## Key Features
- Cascade: AI agentic chatbot for collaborative coding
- Terminal: Enhanced terminal experience
- MCP: Servers that extend agent functionality
- Memories: Customizable behaviors and rules
- Context Awareness: Automatic codebase comprehension
- Workflows: Automation for repetitive development tasks
- App Deploys: Single-click application deployment

## AI Capabilities
- Agentic architecture with autonomous collaboration
- MCP Server integration for extended capabilities
- Memory & Rules system for customization
- Workflow automation

## Platform
- One-click app deployment
- Import VS Code or Cursor settings
- Cross-platform: Mac, Windows, Linux
- Windsurf Next (prerelease access)
- Custom App Icons (beta, Mac only, paid plans)
- Remote server and SSH connection support

## System Requirements
- Mac: OS X Yosemite minimum
- Windows: Windows 10 minimum
- Ubuntu: 20.04+ (glibc >= 2.31, glibcxx >= 3.4.26)
- Other Linux: glibc >= 2.28, glibcxx >= 3.4.25

## Onboarding Process
Four main steps: configuration setup (import from VS Code or Cursor), theme selection, account authentication, and feature introduction.

## Notable Details
- Incompatible with other AI code completion extensions and proprietary extensions
- Keybinding customization (VS Code or Vim options)
- Manual authentication fallback via codes
- Command palette access (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P)
- Uninstallation instructions available for all platforms
